title: Unpinned npm dependency using wildcard or latest
description: An npm dependency uses a wildcard (*), 'latest', or open-ended range (>=) instead of a pinned or caret-restricted version, allowing automatic installation of potentially compromised versions.
remediation: Pin to exact versions or use caret ranges (^). Use package-lock.json and npm ci in CI.

title: Suspicious postinstall script in package.json
description: A package has a postinstall or preinstall lifecycle script that executes during npm install. Malicious packages use these scripts as an initial attack vector.
remediation: Review lifecycle scripts. Use npm install --ignore-scripts and selectively allow trusted scripts.

title: Dependency from unrecognized npm scope
description: A scoped npm package is from an unrecognized organization. While often legitimate, this warrants verification to rule out namespace squatting.
remediation: Verify scope ownership via npm owner ls. Check package download stats and publish history.

title: Package installed via curl pipe to shell
description: Software is installed by piping curl output to a shell, bypassing package manager integrity checks and enabling MITM attacks.
remediation: Download the script first, verify its content and checksum, then execute. Prefer package managers with integrity verification.
